title: PROGRESS.md becomes the minimal human overlay
description: Remove the 'Completed' and 'In progress' sections from PROGRESS.md. Both duplicate state already in features/*.json, which is why PROGRESS.md kept going stale after every PR merge. Keep only the human-content parts: 'Current state' (one line), 'Known issues', 'Next steps'. Reader looking for status runs `npm run harness:features` or reads features/ directly.
verification: PROGRESS.md no longer has '## Completed' or '## In progress' sections
PROGRESS.md retains '## Current state', '## Known issues', '## Next steps'
docs/SESSION.md and AGENTS.md updated to point at features/ for status (not PROGRESS.md)
DECISIONS.md has an entry explaining the trade-off
scripts/harness-ci-checks.sh exits 0 (step 1 still looks for 'Next steps' which we keep)
notes: Direct response to: 'when PRs close, the progress should update.' The cleanest way to stop staleness is to stop duplicating the state in the first place. features/*.json is the single source of truth for feature status. PROGRESS.md becomes ~15 lines of genuinely human content.
